title: Navigator Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Health Care > Navigator Programs 106 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues African American Community AIDS/HIV Issues Drug Use Disorder Issues Hispanic/Latino Community Homelessness Issues Native American Community Pregnant Individuals AIDS/HIV Issues Pregnant Individuals Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness OREGON STATE DIVISION OF MEDICAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS OHP IN-PERSON APPLICATION ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps people find experts who provide in-person assistance with health insurance enrollment. Health Insurance Agents help with private insurance through the Oregon Health Insurance Marketplace (OHIM), while Community Partners, also called assisters, help with enrollment in the Oregon Health Plan (OHP). 500 Summer Street NE E-49 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Varies by service Get Directions Visit Website More Details ADAPT INTEGRATED HEALTH CARE BANDON OUTREACH AND ENROLLMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community outreach and support related to health and wellness. Offers enrollment assistance for OHP and private insurance via the Federal Health Exchange. Help connect residents with medical, dental, and mental health services, as well as food and housing resources. 1010 First Street SE Suite 110 Bandon, OR 97411 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-6pm, Friday 8am-5pm (541) 347-2529 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ADAPT INTEGRATED HEALTH CARE PORT ORFORD OUTREACH AND ENROLLMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community outreach and support related to health and wellness. Offers enrollment assistance for OHP and private insurance via the Federal Health Exchange. Help connect residents with medical, dental, and mental health services, as well as food and housing resources. 1312 Tichenor Avenue Port Orford, OR 97465 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-6pm, Friday 8am-5pm (541) 347-2529 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AFRICAN REFUGEE IMMIGRANT ORGANIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community events such as youth soccer, open gym, tutoring, community-specific workshops (topics include traditions, art, music, history, or language), leadership workshops, and clothing donation drives. Also assists in enrollment in the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) and helps individuals who are job searching, resume building, and with job application form completion. Provides information about rent assistance programs. 7535 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 972133905 Eligibility: Varies by service with a focus on African refugees and immigrant families Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Events: Varies by service Email (971) 430-6193 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AFRICAN YOUTH AND COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION MULTI-COUNTY OFFICE BRIDGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance in navigating disability services, culturally specific advocacy, and referrals to outside organizations as needed. 2110 SE 82nd Avenue Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Ages 9-26 who are on the autism spectrum Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm, Friday by appointment only Email (971) 254-8916 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AFRICAN YOUTH AND COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION WASHINGTON COUNTY OFFICE BRIDGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance in navigating disability services, culturally specific advocacy, and referrals to outside organizations as needed. 6835 SW Clinton Street Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Ages 9-26 who are on the autism spectrum Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm, Friday by appointment only Email (971) 249-8064 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AVIVA HEALTH ROSEBURG AVIVA HEALTH GLIDE HEALTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides primary care, services include care for acute and chronic illnesses, annual exams for children and adults, health screenings, and health education related to patients needs and issues. Offers insurance enrollment assistance. 20170 N Umpqua Highway Glide, OR 97443 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-6pm Email (541) 496-3504 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AVIVA HEALTH ROSEBURG AVIVA HEALTH NORTH COUNTY HEALTH CENTER HEALTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides primary care, services include care for acute and chronic illnesses, annual exams for children and adults, health screenings, and health education related to patients needs and issues. Offers insurance enrollment assistance. 150 Kenneth Ford Drive Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-6pm Email (541) 672-9596 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AVIVA HEALTH ROSEBURG OREGON MOTHERSCARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps pregnant people get connected to prenatal care and other resources in their communities. Services include health insurance navigation, such as OHP application assistance, prenatal care appointment scheduling, health education, and community referrals. Referrals can be provided for WIC, dental care, home visiting programs, and other prenatal support services. 150 Kenneth Ford Drive Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Any pregnant person is eligible for OMC, regardless of their income or insurance status Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 672-9596 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AVIVA HEALTH ROSEBURG AVIVA HEALTH SUTHERLIN HEALTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides primary care, services include care for acute and chronic illnesses, annual exams for children and adults, health screenings, and health education related to patients needs and issues. Referrals can be provided for WIC, dental care, home visiting programs, and other prenatal support services. 2200 4th Street Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Pregnant people Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 523-8211 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BEAVERTON RESOURCE CENTER NAVIGATOR PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists in Oregon Health Plan (OHP) and private insurance enrollment via the Federal Health Exchange. 13565 SW Walker Road Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: -Call Center hours: Monday-Friday 9am-1pm -Walk in hours: Tuesday/Wednesday/ Friday 9am-1pm, Monday/Thursday by appointment only Email (503) 207-5670 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BENTON COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH OREGON MOTHERSCARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Oregon MothersCare provides prenatal assessments to help women begin the path to prenatal care. Services include pregnancy testing, OHP application assistance, streamlined intake/referral for prenatal care, and dental care referrals. Specialized information and referral also available for WIC, home visitation programs, and other prenatal support services. 530 NW 27th Street Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Pregnant women at any stage of pregnancy Hours: Monday/Wednesday 8am-7pm, Tuesday/Thursday/Friday, 8am-5pm (541) 766-6835 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BORLAND FREE CLINIC PRIMARY CARE COMMUNITY CLINIC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Providies a community clinic offering primary and some speciality care. Also offers gynecological services, mental health care, physical therapy, diabetes education classes, prescription medicine discounts, assistance in applying for OHP, referrals to outside providers, and information about other social services. 3550 SW Borland Road Tualatin, OR 97062 Eligibility: Individuals who are at or below 400% of the Federal Poverty Line (FPL) Hours: Monday/Thursday 3pm-7pm, Wednesday 9am-1pm Email (503) 974-8887 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASA LATINOS UNIDOS FAMILY EMPOWERMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides wraparound support, transportation, language interpretation, assistance filling out forms (e.g., W-2s, job applications, rental forms, and appointments with other agencies), navigation and enrollment for the Oregon Health Plan, and assistance with documentation of discrimination. 3460 Midvale Drive Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Members of the Latinx community Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 512-4949 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASA LATINOS UNIDOS LEBANON OFFICE FAMILY EMPOWERMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides wraparound support, transportation, language interpretation, assistance filling out forms (e.g., W-2s, job applications, rental forms, and appointments with other agencies), navigation and enrollment for the Oregon Health Plan, and assistance with documentation of discrimination. 500 Weldwood Drive Building E Lebanon, OR 97355 Eligibility: Members of the Latinx community Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 512-4949 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY HEALTH CENTERS BEAVERCREEK HEALTH CENTER PRIMARY CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides primary medical care for adults and children such as general physicals, vaccinations, treatment of acute and chronic illness, prenatal care, substance use assessment, and health education. Also assists in applying for the Oregon Health Plan and private insurances on the Federal Health Exchange. 110 Beavercreek Road Suite 100 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-7pm Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY HEALTH CENTERS SANDY HEALTH CENTER PRIMARY CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides primary medical care for adults and children such as general physicals, vaccinations, treatment of acute and chronic illness, prenatal care, substance use assessment, and health education. Other services include treatment referrals; safer sex materials (condoms, lube, prep/pep); and health insurance navigation. 9775 SE Sunnyside Road Suite 200 Clackamas, OR 97015 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Thursday 5pm-7pm Email (971) 361-8801 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH SANDY HEALTH CENTER WIC OFFICE OREGON MOTHERSCARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps pregnant people get connected to prenatal care and other resources in their communities. Referrals can be provided for WIC, dental care, home visiting programs, and other prenatal support services. 39740 Pleasant Street Sandy, OR 97055 Eligibility: Pregnant people Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (971) 930-9617 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS SERVICE CENTER MEDICAL SERVICES AND NEEDLE EXCHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mobile medical care and needle exchange by hosting Central City Concern's and Outside In's medical vans and syringe exchange program. Includes a full range of health services such as disease management, routine primary care, HIV/STI testing, women's health care, mental health, and transgender care. Also provides assistance with enrollment in Oregon Health Plan (OHP) or other health insurance plans. For the syringe exchange, users get an even exchange for however many syringes they bring in, though no one will be turned away for not having returns. 8800 SE 80th Avenue Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: -Medical van: Age 13 and older. Low-income individuals and households. Must not be receiving primary care from another provider. -Needle exchange: Ages 18 and older Hours: Medical van: Tuesday 9am-3pm Needle exchange: Tuesday/Thursday 1pm-4pm Email (503) 771-7914 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COALITION OF COMMUNITY HEALTH CLINICS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Coordinates a network of safety net clinics which serve Oregon Health Plan (OHP) members, uninsured individuals, and low-income populations. Services include assistance with OHP enrollment, finding doctors and dentists, and support for basic needs. The Perinatal Care Coordination program is able to provide individual case management during the prenatal and postpartum periods. Services include childbirth education, nurse home visiting, maternal mental health resources and counseling, and information and referral for community resources and infant care supplies. 2577 NE Courtney Drive Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Individuals needing prenatal or postpartum resources Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 322-7400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DESCHUTES COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH DOWNTOWN HEALTH CENTER REDMOND OREGON MOTHERSCARE AND PERINATAL CARE COORDINATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Oregon MothersCare provides prenatal assessments to help women begin the path to prenatal care. Services include childbirth education, nurse home visiting, maternal mental health resources and counseling, and information and referral for community resources and infant care supplies. 236 NW Kingwood Avenue Redmond, OR 97756 Eligibility: Individuals needing prenatal or postpartum resources Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 322-7448 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EUVALCREE EUVALCREE HERMISTON OHP ASSISTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists with Medicaid enrollment and provides information about the Oregon Health Insurance Marketplace. 236 E Newport Avenue Hermiston, OR 97838 Eligibility: Low-income Oregon residents Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-6pm Email (541) 567-1185 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EUVALCREE EUVALCREE ONTARIO OHP ASSISTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists with Medicaid enrollment and provides information about the Oregon Health Insurance Marketplace. 67 SW 2nd Avenue Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Low-income Oregon residents Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-6pm Email (541) 889-3189 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers information about applying for SNAP (food stamps) and the Oregon Health Plan, subsidized housing, counseling and support groups, and other information about local services and programs. Distributes and accepts applications for the Lions Club Sight and Hearing Program, which provides vision and hearing tests, glasses, and hearing aids to low-income individuals who have no other means of obtaining vision or hearing exams and aids. Distributes and accepts applications for dental clinics for low-income individuals without dental insurance or any realistic way of paying for dental care. 3520 Frontage Road Reedsport, OR 97467 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-2pm Email (541) 271-9700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FOSTERCLUB FOSTER YOUTH RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects teens and young adults in and from foster care to resources, people, and organizations that can support basic needs. This includes applying for Medicaid, SNAP, and other services.
